City of Alarneda California
Before the property can be assessed for
the California Water Code requires the City
Office of the City Attorney, Room 314
city Hall
Santa Clara Avenue at Oak Street '94501
4t5.748 4il4

n--
C)li"
..,)
' 
v(-
February 6, L992
Scott Hallabrin
Acting General Counsel
Legal Division
California Fair Political Practices Commission
428 J Street, Suite 800
P.O. Box 807
Sacramento, CA 95804-0807
Re: Request for Formal Written Advice for City Officials and
Staff Regarding Actions Related to Bay Farm Island
Reclamation District No. 2LO5
Dear Mr. Hallabrin:
On behalf of various City officials and staff, I am writing to
request formal written advice frorn the FPPC pursuant to Government
Code Section 83114(b) on the official's 
and staffst ability 
to
participate in decisions of the Alameda City Council related to Bay
Farm Island Reclamation District No. 2LO5. John Wallace of your
office and I discussed the basic issues two days ago. 
The
conclusion was that a conflict probably existed regarding the
maintenance assessment hearing matters.
The City of Alameda is approximately 9.5 sguare miles in area.
The population is 76,459 according to the 1990 census data. 
Bay
Farm fsland Reclamation District No. 2LO5 ("District") 
includes
Iess than one seventh of the Cityts population and is located on a
portion of Bay Farm Island which is separate from the main island.
I have attached a map of the area for your convenience.
The District was established to construct reclamation works
including a perimeter dike and interior drainage system. Funding
for the work was financed by bonds which were paid off by assessing
property within the District's 
boundaries. Construction of the
worX has been completed. Pursuant to its contracts with the City
and permits from Bay Conservation and Development Commission, the
District is reguired to maintain its reclamation works. In order
to accomplish the maintenance, the District 
is proposing. to
establisfr a maintenance assessment on property within the District.
the maintenance work,
to take several


Scott HaIIabrin
February 6, L992
Page 2
actions. Fj-rst, after recej,ving a petition from the District, the
City Council is required to appoint three valuation cornrnissioners.
water Code Section 5L322. (This step has already been done. The
potentially inpacted official 
and staff did not participate in the
decision or its 
process. )
Second, after 
the valuation
commissioners have determined the proposed assessments and the
assessment rol,l is forwarded to the City Council, the Council sets
a time for hearing objections to the assessments. Water Code
Section 5L329. Third, at the hearing the City Council considers
any written 
objections and hears evidence supporting the
objections. The city Council may modify the assessments. Water
Code Section 5l-332. Fourth, the City Council orders approval of
the assessments or nodified assessments. water Code Section
5L332.1.
The Mayor, City Manager, City Attorney and Finance Director
all own homes in the District and are subject to any potential
assessments. They have abstained to date from any action regarding
the maintenance assessments including the appointment of the
valuation commissioners. I am asking on their behalf to $ihat
extent these City officials 
rnay participate in future actions
related to the hearing on the maintenance assessments, i.e. the
second, third and fourth steps outlined above.
I would appreciate your response as soon as possible. 
In
order to meet the countyrs deadline for itens to be included on the
tax ro1ls, the city Council must take action on these reclamation
district 
issues no later than May 19, L992. If you need any
additional inforrnation, please feel free to contact me at (510)
748-4544. Thank you for your assistance.
Very Truly Yours,
$rd'\/A*,^!)
Ebather-t. Mc"Ladq/hlin
Assistant City Attorney
enclosure
cc: Mayor and City Council
City Attorney
City Manager
Ass istant City l,tanager
Finance Director
